UIXCreateLiveEngine

Creates the main Xbox Drop-in UI engine.

HRESULT UIXCreateLiveEngine(
    LPCSTR pSkinFileName,
    DWORD LanguageID,
    ILiveEngine **ppEngine
);

Parameters

pSkinFileName
[in] Pointer to a null-terminated string specifying the file name of the skin file (.uix file) that the engine will use.
LanguageID
[in] Language identifier for the language that the engine will use for text. See XGetLanguage for a list of possible values.
ppEngine
[out] Address of an ILiveEngine pointer that receives the Xbox Drop-in UI engine interface.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

Create a Direct3D device (with IDirect3D8::CreateDevice) before calling UIXCreateLiveEngine, or an assert will fire.

The SkinBld tool generates a skin (.uix) file from a text skin description file (.inx file). A default description file (default.inx) is included with the XDK.

The language specified in LanguageID determines which sections in the skin file will be used. If a section with a matching language is defined, that section will be used. Otherwise, default language sections (sections with no language specified) will be used. The language identifiers map to the two-letter language codes used in the skin file. (Note that XC_LANGUAGE_PORTUGUESE maps to 'BR', Brazil.)

To free the Xbox Live engine interface when it is no longer needed, call Release on the ILiveEngine pointer.

For titles that have been auto-updated, the title must verify that the skin file passed in pSkinFileName has not been modified. If the skin file resides on the title's game disc, verifying the file signature is not necessary. If the title has been auto-updated, however, the skin file could reside on the T: drive (a writeable storage location), and the file content signature must be verified before the file is used. See Content Signatures.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Uix.h.

Import Library: Use Uix.lib.

See Also

ILiveEngine, XGetLanguage, ILiveEngine::SetUIPlugin
